LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY, LLP'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT CITE A COGNIZABLE BASIS TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT IS REJECTED BECAUSE IT WAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY AND PLAINTIFF IS NOT SEEKING TO ENFORCE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT FEE AGREEMENT FOR ITS BENEFIT. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 19, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JUNE 10, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FILED A STATEMENT OF NON-OPPOSITION FILED ON JUNE 1, 2012. THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE MOVING PARTY. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF MUST ARBITRATE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT HWANG. THE REST OF THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFF IS NOT COMPELLED TO ARBITRATE ITS CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY, LLP. THERE IS NO ARBITRATION AGREEMENT BETWEEN THESE PARTIES AND THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT DE LA PENA DO NOT ARISE FROM THE CONTRACT AND CAN BE DETERMINED INDEPENDENT OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE CONTRACTUAL CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE LAW FIRM OF DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY LLP'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG IS GRANTED FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE WAS PRESENTED AT THE HEARING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BONNIE L. PORTIS OF DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY LLP, TEL. NO. 268-8133, MOVING PARTY;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE LAW FIRM OF DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY LLP'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG IS GRANTED FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE WAS PRESENTED AT THE HEARING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is Continued to June 19, 2013 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 10, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Reynolds, Esq., of De La Pena & Holiday LLP, Tel. 415.288.8000, for Plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG's Petition To Compel Arbitration is Granted in part and Denied in part. Plaintiff must arbitrate the breach of contract claims against Defendant Hwang. The rest of the case is stayed pending arbitration. Plaintiff is not compelled to arbitrate its claims against Defendant De La Pena & Holiday, LLP. There is no arbitration agreement between these parties and the doctrine of equitable estoppel does not apply because Plaintiff's claims against Defendant De La Pena do not arise from the contract and can be determined independent of the issues raised by the contractual claims. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Plaintiff filed a statement of non-opposition filed on June 1, 2012. The court signed the proposed order submitted by the moving party. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DE LA PENA & HOLIDAY, LLP'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.
Appearances: Christine C. Lee of McLennon Law Corporation (394.6688) for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
No other appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant's moving papers do not cite a cognizable basis to compel arbitration. The equitable estoppel argument is rejected because it was raised for the first time in the reply and plaintiff is not seeking to enforce the attorney-client fee agreement for its benefit. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
